name: PagerTree Incident Alerting API
what_it_does: Incident alerting and on-call management platform with REST API — routing alerts via phone, SMS, email, Slack, and Teams with on-call scheduling, escalation policies, and status pages as a budget-friendly PagerDuty alternative.
use_cases: Routing monitoring alerts to on-call engineers via multiple channels through REST API
Managing on-call schedules with calendar-based rotations programmatically
Creating incidents from CI/CD pipelines or monitoring tools via inbound webhooks
Building status page updates linked to incident lifecycle via API
Integrating PagerTree with 100+ monitoring tools via pre-built integrations
not_for: Complex SRE workflows with automated postmortem generation
Teams needing enterprise-grade SLA guarantees from large vendor
Deep ITSM integration requiring ServiceNow or Jira SM complexity
best_when: A small to medium engineering team needs affordable on-call alerting with multi-channel notifications and on-call management at a fraction of PagerDuty cost.
avoid_when: You need enterprise SRE features, complex reliability workflows, or are a large team needing vendor SLA commitment.

auth.notes: API token from PagerTree account settings. Bearer token authentication. Account-scoped access. Integration tokens for inbound alert sources. Token management in dashboard.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free: 4 users, unlimited alerts, basic features
pricing.notes: Very affordable vs. PagerDuty. Free tier with 4 users — good for small teams. Bootstrapped company with long product history. Transparent pricing. Good for startups and SMBs.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits per plan; not prominently documented

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API v4 covers alerts, teams, schedules, and integrations. Webhook support for alert events. Good integration catalog with 100+ pre-built connectors. API documentation improving with v4 rewrite.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Alert creation creates new alerts. Alert deduplication via dedup key. Schedule queries are idempotent reads. Status updates are idempotent for same values.
known_agent_gotchas: API v4 is current — older v3 endpoints being deprecated; use v4 for new integrations
Phone call alerts use third-party providers — verify coverage for your region
On-call schedule override requires separate API call — not part of schedule update
Status page linked to alerts — status updates automated if alert-status-page link configured

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. Bearer token auth. GDPR compliant. US data residency. Smaller company — fewer formal security certifications than enterprise vendors. Integration webhook signatures for inbound security.
